root_text.an4.70:1.1: “Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye rājāno adhammikā honti, rājāyuttāpi tasmiṁ samaye adhammikā honti.
root_text.an4.70:1.2: Rājāyuttesu adhammikesu brāhmaṇagahapatikāpi tasmiṁ samaye adhammikā honti.
root_text.an4.70:1.3: Brāhmaṇagahapatikesu adhammikesu negamajānapadāpi tasmiṁ samaye adhammikā honti.
root_text.an4.70:1.5: Visamaṁ candimasūriyesu parivattantesu visamaṁ nakkhattāni tārakarūpāni parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:1.6: Visamaṁ nakkhattesu tārakarūpesu parivattantesu visamaṁ rattindivā parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:1.7: Visamaṁ rattindivesu parivattantesu visamaṁ māsaddhamāsā parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:1.8: Visamaṁ māsaddhamāsesu parivattantesu visamaṁ utusaṁvaccharā parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:1.9: Visamaṁ utusaṁvaccharesu parivattantesu visamaṁ vātā vāyanti visamā apañjasā.
root_text.an4.70:1.10: Visamaṁ vātesu vāyantesu visamesu apañjasesu devatā parikupitā bhavanti.
root_text.an4.70:1.11: Devatāsu parikupitāsu devo na sammā dhāraṁ anuppavecchati.
root_text.an4.70:1.12: Deve na sammā dhāraṁ anuppavecchante visamapākāni sassāni bhavanti.
root_text.an4.70:1.13: Visamapākāni, bhikkhave, sassāni manussā paribhuñjantā appāyukā honti dubbaṇṇā ca bavhābādhā ca.
root_text.an4.70:2.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye rājāno dhammikā honti, rājāyuttāpi tasmiṁ samaye dhammikā honti.
root_text.an4.70:2.2: Rājāyuttesu dhammikesu brāhmaṇagahapatikāpi tasmiṁ samaye dhammikā honti.
root_text.an4.70:2.3: Brāhmaṇagahapatikesu dhammikesu negamajānapadāpi tasmiṁ samaye dhammikā honti.
root_text.an4.70:2.5: Samaṁ candimasūriyesu parivattantesu samaṁ nakkhattāni tārakarūpāni parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:2.6: Samaṁ nakkhattesu tārakarūpesu parivattantesu samaṁ rattindivā parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:2.7: Samaṁ rattindivesu parivattantesu samaṁ māsaddhamāsā parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:2.8: Samaṁ māsaddhamāsesu parivattantesu samaṁ utusaṁvaccharā parivattanti.
root_text.an4.70:2.9: Samaṁ utusaṁvaccharesu parivattantesu samaṁ vātā vāyanti samā pañjasā.
root_text.an4.70:2.10: Samaṁ vātesu vāyantesu samesu pañjasesu devatā aparikupitā bhavanti.
root_text.an4.70:2.11: Devatāsu aparikupitāsu devo sammā dhāraṁ anuppavecchati.
root_text.an4.70:2.12: Deve sammā dhāraṁ anuppavecchante samapākāni sassāni bhavanti.
root_text.an4.70:2.13: Samapākāni, bhikkhave, sassāni manussā paribhuñjantā dīghāyukā ca honti vaṇṇavanto ca balavanto ca appābādhā cāti.

translation_text.an4.70:1.1: “At a time when kings are unprincipled, royal officials become unprincipled.
translation_text.an4.70:1.2: When royal officials are unprincipled, brahmins and householders become unprincipled.
translation_text.an4.70:1.3: When brahmins and householders are unprincipled, the people of town and country become unprincipled.
translation_text.an4.70:1.4: When the people of town and country are unprincipled, the courses of the moon and sun become erratic.
translation_text.an4.70:1.5: … the courses of the stars and constellations …
translation_text.an4.70:1.8: the seasons and years become erratic.
translation_text.an4.70:1.9: … the blowing of the winds becomes erratic and chaotic.
translation_text.an4.70:1.11: … the heavens don’t provide enough rain.
translation_text.an4.70:1.13: When people eat crops that have ripened erratically, they become short-lived, ugly, weak, and sickly.
translation_text.an4.70:2.1: At a time when kings are principled, royal officials become principled.
translation_text.an4.70:2.3: people of town and country become principled.
translation_text.an4.70:2.4: When the people of town and country are principled, the courses of the sun and moon become regular.
translation_text.an4.70:2.8: the seasons and years become regular.
translation_text.an4.70:2.9: … the blowing of the winds becomes regular and orderly.
translation_text.an4.70:2.11: … the heavens provide plenty of rain.
translation_text.an4.70:2.12: When the heavens provide plenty of rain, the crops ripen well.
translation_text.an4.70:2.13: When people eat crops that have ripened well, they become long-lived, beautiful, strong, and healthy.
translation_text.an4.70:3.2: if the bull goes off course,
translation_text.an4.70:3.4: because their leader is off course.
translation_text.an4.70:4.4: what do you expect the rest to do?
translation_text.an4.70:6.4: what do you expect the rest to do?
translation_text.an4.70:6.5: The whole country sleeps at ease,

variant_text.an4.70:1.12: visamapākāni → visamapākīni (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); visamaṁ pākāni (mr)
variant_text.an4.70:6.3: sace → so ceva (bj, pts1ed); so ce (sya-all)
